channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: XRP is the biggest scam ever — 31 replies — 106.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP is the biggest scam ever

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-04-24 23:45 UTC

OP

they sell token to shift money over to the tradfi company Ripple to buy actual companies and assets and don't pay any dividends or yield on actual xrp tokens. I've seen actual xrp baggies say this is somehow a good thing.

Early replies (first 3)

62143238
Gee, wonder what they could buy

62143321
>>62143203
Means I can buy more for less. BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62143438
>>62143203
>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what

hypocrites and mind raped baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62143537
Biggest and longest running scam in crypto
I sold my ripple shares in 2017 and never looked back

62143560
>>62143438
>>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
uses the funds to support their protocol
>>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what
uses the funds to support their protocol

>hypocrites and mind raped baggies
the other buys companies and ignores their own blockchain then gas lights their own community and claims that "xrp is the center of everything they do"

62143593
>>62143203
Did they pass the Clarity Act yet? No? When are they gonna pass it? Nobody knows? So, same as yesterday?

62143907
>>62143438
Someone post the alts chipmunk.

62144675
>>62143203
Seething linkbaggie
how is that flippening coming along zach?
https://link-flip.alwaysbeshipp.ing/

62144998
>>62143203
>chainlinkdog obsesses over biggest most obvious scam ever that does the same thing as the company he works for
interesting
would be a real shame if xrp went up in price especially compared to link
I wonder how they will cope then

62145068
>>62143560
so ripple buying prime brokerage and countless other institutional leaning companies to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community? crypto tribal fags are so pathetic

62146230
>>62143438
>>62144998
>>62145068
Ripple dumps on TOKEN HOLDERS to fund things that DO NOT USE THE TOKEN, and explicitly only benefit SHAREHOLDERS.

Nobody else in crypto does this.
How are you people so insanely retarded.

62146371
>>62146230
Chainlink does the exact same thing you dense paid mouthbreather shill
None of the shit that CLL promotes or builds or advertizes has anything to do whit the token itself

62146402
>>62146371
If it uses CL nodes, it uses the token.
So to prove your position, all you have to do is name one CLL product that does not use the nodes.
Go

62146438
>>62146402
name one instance when you had to purchase the token
go

62146458
>>62146371
>it has nothing to do with the token

>>62146438
>ok it does need the token, but you didn't purchase it directly

woosh go the goalposts

62146481
>>62146458
>muh subsidies mean token needed despite the fact that absolutely no one buys it or uses it for anything
you're unironically a moron if you truly believe semantics and nuances are doing anything for you
you've been trying this angle for 6 years to no avail, read the room you spastic

62146485
>>62146371
>it has nothing to do with the token

>>62146438
>ok it does need the token, but you didn't purchase it directly

>>62146481
>ok purchasing directly or indirectly is irrelevant, but some of it is subsidized

Second goalpost move in a row.

62146502
>>62146485
not a single product requires the usage of LINK
no, subsidies do not matter at all, sorry
not a single trial or pilot requires the LINK token at any capacity, not a single institutional partner has ever touched the token
can't wait until you'll finally get doxed and raped in Minecraft

62146506
>>62146502
>not a single product requires the usage of LINK
All products use CL nodes, therefore requiring the usage of Link.

You're probably going to loop right back to "but you didn't purchase the Link directly".

>no, subsidies do not matter at all, sorry
But you're the one who brought up the subsidies.

What a shitty chatbot.

62146532
>>62146506
you do realize that once Chainlink implodes, you along with all the other paid marketeers will be in grave danger? you do realize that attacking your entire online persona around a crypto scamcoin will inevitably end bad for you?
tick, tock...

62146544
>>62146532
>paid marketeer
I'm not the one lying, moving goalposts, forgetting my own talking points, and changing IDs.

Go ahead, name one CL product that does not use CL nodes (and therefore the Link token).

62146565
>>62146506
kill yourself before I find you, Rajesh

62146578
>says some bullshit
>gets debunked
>changes IDs and gets extremely emotional

many such cases, sad!

62147333
>>62145068
>to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community?
What ecosystem? The one they claim is coming? None of the companies they bought have integrated XRPL.

62147341
>>62143203
>dont come to /biz/ for a year
>its still the same fucking threads by cultist linkies and xrp schizos
We’ll see you next year

62147491
>>62147341
it's literally the same 4 retards in every fucking thread

62147758
>>62146458
literally the same can be said of xrp kek

62147964
Chainlink is such a pathetic little project...

62148311
>>62147341
next year chainlink will be dead and buried though, and the catalogue will be flooded with frogposters crying about "why didn't you tell me????"

62151224
>>62147758
Fucking how?

62154431
>>62146230
Ripple don't sell to retail and their products are building around actual use. Chainlink sell every green candle directly to retail and built zero utility for the token that is itself completely disconnected from the actual product.

item.title: /smg/ - Super Mega Gunt — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - Super Mega Gunt

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-04-25 00:00 UTC

OP

Ralphamarket Edition
>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>NEW THREAD: Copy-paste the following when making a new thread:
https://pastebin.com/GfB48W5G

Previously on /smg/
>>62153465

Early replies (first 3)

62154402
This one is 15 seconds earlier than the other one

62154405
FIVE STAR DAYS BISH

62154406
>>62154392
how is this butt-thumber still alive ffs? TGD

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62154409
> futures

62154410
>>62154402
he timed the smg market better than me

62154413
>>62154410
left me a thread holder

62154446
>>62154402
Good enough.

62154455
Grok gen me an image of a bull choking a bear like Itachi choking Sasuke

62154470
Post your top 5 allocations

62154474
Will russia getting rekt affect markets?

62154479
>>62154470
hopefully you are an old retired man

62154485
>>62154474
It will mean more of an oil shock.
As we've seen, this is good for markets.

item.title: DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-24 23:59 UTC

OP

DO YOU KNOW ANYONE WHO LOST MONEY ON MEMECOINS

Early replies (first 3)

62154328
Yeah

62154329
>>62154323
Almost everyone lost money on memecoins just like how she lost all her gains

62154342
>>62154323
I want her to squeeze my head like a pumpkin in between her thighs, if you catch my meaning.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62154347
>>62154323
I know a guy. I also knew a guy who lost his money on futures. Same story for both. Decided they'd rather day trade from a bar than get a real job. Wounds up broke, alone, and alcoholics.

62154361
>>62154323
memecoins are made for losing money. you have to balance out your capital gains somehow

62154373
>>62154323
Me. Is this a trick question? This is /biz/, most people here lost money on memecoins

62154377
>>62154361
This. You need to get some losses out of your system. Need to get a cheap thrill once in a while with some retarded small bets so that you stay rational with your core folio

62154462
>>62154347
>>62154329
>>62154328
>>62154373
are you guys being serious or are you trolling?

Almost everyone on x that I see is still hyped on memecoins
Can you share more stories? I'm not part of gen z and don't really know what it's like on the ground for you guys

62154473
>>62154323
>yeah lost money last few days
>still losing
>she's cute ngl
>arms are a bit much not my thing

62154481
I know him, and he is me.

item.title: Food for thought — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Food for thought

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-24 23:57 UTC

OP

Oil after unilateral ceasefire announced: 82

Oil after unilateral ceasefire ended: 95

Oil after one sided “ceasefire” announced: 97

Oil after one sided “ceasefire” ended (today thru weekend): 105

Gasoline: war ATH
jet fuel: war ATH

There is no exit with peace. Trump can announce a 1 month ceasefire, but if the strait remains blocked, oil will be 150 by then. There MUST and WILL be another phase of war. There is an Iran put on both oil and volatility. Be wary shorting either of them. Additionally, there are no more hedges. If war happens again, it won’t be the -1.7% days, it’ll be -3.7%.

Do with this as you will

Early replies (first 3)

62154445
>>62154427
The way this ends is the US goes home, simple as. They can do it now or they can do it after they wasted most of their munitions.

62154447
where is US nat gas?

also heating oil in the summer? who cares lol

62154457
>>62154447
Typically places like Europe buy a lot of things during summer to stock up for the winter at a cheaper price. They will not be able to get discount off-season prices this year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62154466
>>62154445
The us will leave the strait under iranian control sure

62154468
>>62154466
The US has no choice. They will also abandon their former bases along the Persian Gulf. Do try and keep up, kid.

62154472
>>62154457
europes leaders work very hard to increase their energy prices so they should be happy about this. shutting down nukes, cancelling off shore oil drilling, adding taxes to everything.

their gas has cost more than US for forever. they like it.

62154475
>>62154472
Euro leaders will end up with their heads on pikes

item.title: TICK TOCK — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
TICK TOCK

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-24 23:54 UTC

OP

TICK TOCK

Early replies (first 3)

62153825
Who gives a fuck about crypto? Do you retards have any idea what the implications are here? You have government agencies storing ALL data transferred over the internet. That is soon to be unencrypted. Think of what this means for every government, business, and individuals having 100% of their private data exposed.

62153839
>>62153776
15 bits, ooooooo, you can brute force 15 bits on traditional hardware easy

62153852
>project eleven
it's a scam lol
they fed it the answer
this is probably one of the most pathetic quantum scams of the decade
extremely low effort

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62153853
>>62153839
In case anyone doesn't know, 15 bits = 2^15 = 32768 combinations. You could crack that with a handful of monkeys rolling dice.

62153858
Muh crypto is the least concern if ECC is broken.

62153863
you nigger scam is too easy to expose now that we have claude

62154465
>>62153776
wooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo
now do it with the 256 bit key
to give you fuckers an idea: if you tested trillions of value per seconds and that you spent the next trillion year testing keys non stop, you'd have effectively tested the equivalent of a rounding error of the total number of keys

item.title: Weed Stocks — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Weed Stocks

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-24 23:50 UTC

OP

What ones are you bros going into?

I chose ACB, Aurora Cannabis as it's the only one that has made a significant profit this year.

Peak - 1503.00
Currently - 3.34

Early replies (first 3)

62152347
>>62151907
Bump. This board is just crypto gamblers isn't it?

62152480
>>62152347
bullshit, this board is for moni makn

62152857
>>62151907
I made huge bank on Canopy and tilray when Canada went legal.
I think tilray is better posed right now for usa and europe.
usa is still long way off federally.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152931
>>62151907
yeah long personality disorders

62153098
>>62152931
Cope

Epstein even mentions how weed smokers are harder to manipulate.
Tranny redd*tors cause more mental illness then maraijuana

62154452
>>62152931
Yea because pills didn't make people into troons or anything. Sleeping pills went so well.

Stupid goy.

>>62153098
I believe this in a way. You are more cerebral and definitely more heart motivated than head motivated. Pot heads are retards but it's definitely helpful and powerful in the right moments.

item.title: Look at this fucking chart lmao — 10 replies — 16.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Look at this fucking chart lmao

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-24 23:26 UTC

OP

This is literally worse than the final dotcom days

Good things all mag7 report next week, which either:

1. Increase capex and mag7 dump again
2. Decrease capex and BAKA dumps
2. Miss earnings and AI is proven ineffective, BAKA dumps, mag7 dumps

PCE next week, FOMC next week, Iran ceasefire ends next week

Exciting times to be long volatility!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62153883
the war caused the market to dump when it should have been at ath because all the players in it are crushing it to never before seen levels. all you're seeing is a correction to what would have happened over a longer period of time if not for the war meme which will probably due some more damage before it's done sure whatever le dotcom bubble

62153897
>>62153883

CPI is currently running 4% lil bro, and Hormuz 2nd order effects aren’t even here yet, EPS estimates keep getting ratcheted up. You are 1 employment report from 5.5% on the 30 year. You wanna run 2.5% real GDP and 5% inflation with yields at 4%? Okay, good luck with that bet, because longing dogshit bets you’re betting rates are only going down from here

Best bull case is Warsh hikes his first FOMC. If he cuts, market dumps 15%

62153919
>>62153897
dips are gonna get eaten up. you're looking too much into external factors. the main players are crushing it. the external factors cause temporary overreactions that turn into an easy buying opportunity and not much else

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62153927
>>62153919

That’s a lot of words to say nothing.

The second inflation expectations pick up, rate hike probabilities go up, and that’s all it takes for a market to trade like 2022. So again, if you think the government running crazy fiscal deficits, inflation running 5-6%, companies dumping into capex, and easing FCI is going to mean LOWER rates, good luck to your book

62153935
>>62153927
your brain keeps growing and your bank account keeps shrinking

62153996
>>62153935

Who said I was short equities? Stick to your day job and keep DCAing. You’ll retire at 55 like a good boy

62153999
>>62153828
op is a brokie

62154002
>>62153897
Didn't read
Money printer goes brrrr

62154004
>>62153935
they have to convince themselves they have gained something due to the financial losses lol

62154359
>>62153996
yes I'll make and you'll lose everything and

item.title: My Litecoin Strategic Reserve now holds 14.08 LTC — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
My Litecoin Strategic Reserve now holds 14.08 LTC

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-04-25 00:02 UTC

OP

My Litecoin Strategic Reserve now holds 14.08 LTC

Early replies (first 3)

62154028
Can I get a QRD on LITS? I’ve been out of crypto for a year now and thinking about starting to stack again this summer but only stacking 50/50 BTC/LTC as I firmly believe that LTC will pump earlier then BTC this time as the LTC halving is approaching next summer one year before BTC halving. Should I put 50% of my LTC allocation in LITS instead?

62154490
>>62154028
I don't know

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

Why you haven`t bought NVDX? Do you hate get rich?

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-04-24 23:46 UTC

OP

Why you haven`t bought NVDX? Do you hate get rich?

Early replies (first 3)

62154272
>>62154258
we're due for a pullback

62154436
Hadn't heard about it before. Will DYOR.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 85 replies — 25.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-04-24 20:40 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62137662
Soon we shall reach the nirvana of 75$ my Mumbai brothers. Then we shall eat with silver spoons.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62139771
You are not the body, you are not the mind, you are not your emotions. You are the silent DOT in the infinity of consciousness. You are just a wave in the ocean of consciousness that forgot it is part of the ocean. For when rememberance dawns, the mystery is not solved. It is lived.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62141372
My brethren, the time is nigh. We're break out. The wave of consciousness is breaking free from the ocean floor.

62143550
Any second now BTC will erupt into a storm that has never been seen before. DOT will soon start it's ascension to the heavens for 75$+. We, the Mumbai warriors, shall eat with silver spoons very soon. The time is coming my warriors. You still have to to buy a big fat bag of DOT before you get priced out.

62143825
>>62129518
>>62131415
>>62131658
>>62131731
>>62131777
>>62135297
>>62139771
>>62143550

This is sad. Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement

62144010
>>62143825
>This is sad.
My brother, the sadness is in your own heart. Not mine. You are destroying yourself.

>Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement
My brother, I am the one and only Vishnu Kumar. Born and raised in India. I am brown as the mud itself. There is no denying it. I am a true Mumbai warrior. I am a proud Indian.
Also Panjali, didn't people teach you that questions are fundamental for a healthy conversation. My young Panjali, I am not here for engagement. If you asked I would've told you so. Oh young Panjali. You still have a long way ahead of you. If accept, I let go, I move on and I forgive you young Panjali.

Thank you for you kind bump of the thread Panjali. I shall later come to your house and give you your share of the profits.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62146419
My fellow Mumbai warriors. We are all already enlightened, we just need to uncover it. DOT is already the best chain, people just need to realize it. They're still looking at the shadow of their light and misidentifying it for themselves. Soon they shall become aware of it and pivot towards the light.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62148896
100$ end of bull run. Remember my Mumbai warriors. It is still cheap as dirt itself. You want to buy now, before you the GIGA ULTRA MEGA pump happens and you get priced out.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62149307
>>62148896
And then have this larping indian short you

62151325
>>62149307
Pssssstttttt Kamal. Do you want to eat with your hands again or do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat like true kings? With spoons? Young warrior Kamal, you have still much to learn.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62153932
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 56 replies — 22.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-04-24 21:36 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146523
>>62145244
Lmao

62146529
>>62146497
Defi protocols this year are brutal so why still choose this shit

62146577
>>62145154
People will expect you to be Santa everyday

62146661
>>62145144
Is it expensive?

62146671
>>62146529
Better safe than sorry goys

62147207
>>62146497
Yeah it’s safe since you stay in self custody while staking BTC for yield. Look into Babylon if you want to see how it actually works.

62147680
>>62146661
I think the jacket was $1000 lel

62148933
>>62146529
Yeah it’s been brutal, but not all protocols are like that. Bitcoin itself is secured by a strong base layer design. It’s not about chasing yield, it’s about using BTC without breaking its core security or custody model.

62148936
Never tell anyone how much money you have. Poor, rich, anything.

62148947
>>62141874
What makes you think that?

62149370
>>62146661
Like >>62147680 it's was around $1000

62149399
>>62142226
Did you enjoy losing 3 mil, fucker?

62149941
>>62138453
Only my brother knows I bought BTC, we both stake together now as well, with native staking gaining traction. Imagine if I told my parents to buy at 120K Kek.

62150719
>>62142197
You never know

62150903
>>62148933
Still it has risks

62151073
>>62146671
I get that, but it’s better to check first before saying that. So far, it fits my standards. I don’t think firms like Ledger or even A16z would back it otherwise.

62151201
>>62148936
Greedy bastard

62151260
>>62151073
it's the same thing. Everything will fail in the end. Nothing survive

62151310
NEVER

62151368
>>62149941
Heartaches

62151461
>>62138453
How do we confess them?

62151818
>>62149941
Nice are you also waiting for the TBV launch? More interesting for me since it could unlock more use cases.

62152719
>>62142226
That's the goal lol

62152732
never ever tell anyone you own any crypto holy shit that is like rule #1 in life don't tell me you idiots are trying to orange pill your fucking friends and family

62152763
>>62138453
Why would you ever brag about your money? Especially when every cryptobro is a scrawny geek. Do you want to get tortured by niggers for your private key?

Secret wealthy is better

62153574
>>62150903
Every system has risk. The difference is just what kind of risk you’re exposed to and how much control you still keep over your assets.

62153589
>>62152732
Maybe it depends on who you’re closest to in your family. There’s usually one person you really vibe with.

62153929
>>62152763
Do what you wanna do

62154078
>>62142464
This here is the right play. Don't tell anyone how much money you have or flaunt it. It should be used to give you freedom not ego validation. Also when you reveal wealth to people they will try to use you and get some for themselves. Donate to charity and volunteer also though of course.

62154092
If you talk about bitcoin, people either assume you’re a sucker, a drug dealer, evading taxation, or a pedophile. I think what really limits its reach is that it’s technology, economics and political philosophy wrapped into an asset. Never selling.

item.title: Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe… — 52 replies — 13.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe…

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-04-24 20:08 UTC

OP

Today, some guy said to my friend
>you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky
My friend goes
>wtf does that even mean? How come your parents aren’t rich? Are your parents stupid? You make it seem like being rich is based on luck and not intelligence. Does that mean your parents aren’t stupid AND unlucky?
I have noticed a trend of the have-nots trying to pretend sons and daughters of rich parents “didn’t earn the money.”
Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
I noticed the bottom 90% pretend it was just luckto make themselves feel better.
Like come on, even your parents aren’t not rich which means you come from bad stock.
Like okay, sure, “daddy’s money.” How come your dad is poor?
Thoughts, /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

62144840
>median HOUSEHOLD income
Why do they lie so much
A 22 year old in 1985 earning 20k is objectively different from a 22yo in 2023 earning 45k.

And median household income, at a time when people got married earlier is completely different from the median now where people get married later. So 22yos are actually even poorer, usually starting around 30k.

They lie so much it's no wonder people can't afford shit.

62144845
>>62144825
I swear people who say “daddy’s money” are poor. Like lets say someone is rich and has kids, would that person ever say “daddy’s money?”
It would not even make sense.
Like “mom’s basement” is another insult working class brokies use.

62144850
>>62144801
vote for open borders to add more illegals to compete with for housing and jobs. illegals obviously dont hurt the working class in any way ;)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146615
>>62144801
>Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
Yeah sure but how does it benefit me?
Trump's son having "earned his money" with tips about oil mooning doesn't benefit me

62146619
>>62145534
How does what you're saying benefit him?
If you're gonna be useless then there's no reason for you to say anything. If you have nothing to say then shut up.

62146960
>>62146619
>OP asks for advice
>OP gets advice
>(you) nooooo you can't do that this isn't /adv/
OP's not gonna fuck you no matter how hard you simp for him, faggot.

62146991
I don't know OP, there is a saying "he was born on 3rd base so he thinks he hit a home run".

62147007
>>62144976
yes, its generally by poor brown people. They are as obsessed with counting other peoples money as kikes are.

62147036
the average American makes 115k after taxes. the average Amercian houshold takes home 250k.

62147062
>>62146960
>>OP gets advice
He's probably still waiting

62148071
>>62144801
My family isn't poor but not rich either. I make a good wagie wage of $150k/yr in my late 30s, comparable to what my dad made adjusted for inflation, but I am still a wagie. It isn't luck. Some of it is environment, some of it may even be genetics, but assuming your parents had you and raised you intentionally it's due to their actions and decisions that you are where you are and subsequently your grandparents and so on.

I did not receive any direct financial help from my parents apart from them giving me a good classical education that was homeschool when I was little and then a private high school. That is an advantage over just sticking your kids in public school as it meant I was able to have my college education mostly paid for with grants and did much better than average there which lead to good internships and a jump start on a career.

I also have parents who stayed together their whole lives. They provide a safety net I can fall back on temporarily if things go a little wrong without having to worry about being financially rekt. While I haven’t made use of this personally a couple of my siblings have and stayed with them while getting back on their feet. What this has done for me personally however is enable me to take some risks to increase my own position without worry. I'm also involved in my local church community thanks to my parents which is where I found my wife, where I found other job opportunities, and even smaller things like just being able to get a lot of items like furniture, baby stuff, etc for free, having people to split a whole cow for beef with, baby sitters, piano lessons for the kids, stuff like that. This is a type of wealth that is unquantifiable but has a marked impact on your ability to thrive and progress. So even though I didn't get a free million dollars from my parents or anything like that, the way they raised me and the environment they put me in set me up to also be solidly middle class.

62148084
>>62148071
Genetically speaking there is spousal choice, another kind of unquantifiable factor that affects not just intelligence, but also physical capabilities, looks, etc. It's not random, it's not luck, it's a choice.

I am doing the same things for my kids. They may not become ultra wealthies, but I can at least give them every advantage in becoming little chads and making it to the upper half of wagies.

62148207
>>62144801
Technically they didn’t earn but that doesn’t mean the aren’t entitled to it.
My kids will get a healthy inheritance, I think that’s one of the responsibilities of parenthood.

62148298
>>62144801
BRING OUT THE GUILLOTINE

62148321
>THATS BECAUSE POVERTY IS A CHOICE

62148369
>>62144801
Sometimes its skill, sometimes its luck, most often its a combination of both. And on top of that you need to be driven by ambition (ie. have high T levels).

62148493
>>62145262
>but also success was much more nepotism & regionally based 1-2 generations ago
The location part is a huge factor that very few people talk about nowadays. Back in the day there was very little chance to leave you area if you were poor and needed to help your parents around and no internet meant that all the wealth was built up on specific cities depending on the field of work instead of being spread around a bit like nowadays.
But really another huge factor is that most people in the past didn't try to get wealthy because of a combination of religion and not needing to, since life was so easy mode that you could live very comfortably unless you were dirt poor.
There was very little reason to try and make it past the average and, even though it was easier than now at the right places (in part because location gatekept workforce competition as mentioned before), it still required a lot of effort unless you were a nepo baby, so most just found a level where they were comfortable and chilled there.
Now the entire middle class is getting squeezed out, so you either make it or fall down, and people have become obsessed about monetary wealth to the point of mental illness because of this. The average zoomer would rather risk his current standard of living for a chance to make it into a higher class than remaining the same and watching how they get slowly pushed into a lower class than they started at.
This is why everyone is gambling on stocks or prediction markets and why people go en masse into proffessions like software engineering and law where the average is kinda mid but the top 10% make crazy money, even if they don't have any actual interest on those fields of work and chances are they will make the same money as any other educated job they could have liked more.
All generations are a product of their environment when growing up, and the way the current young gens are growing up is tailor made to turn them into sociopathic gamblers.

62148782
>>62144801
Income, and deriving an "income" is largely tied to business and their desire to hire people at a 1) livable wage and 2) not hire down or eliminate positions as they have for the last 30-40 years.

Having a job does not guarantee stability or success as corporations have continuously eroded the cost of labor by a) outsourcing b) offshoring c) making 1 person responsible for 5 peoples jobs d) importing foreign labor (h1b's etc) to erode wages even further. and e) union busting to ensure that nobody in the working class can rally for better pay, working conditions, stability, overtime pay etc...

basically, for the last 40 years large corporations (usually multi-nationals) have conspired together to erode wages and destroy the working class. who are now being displaced and replaced with foreign labor, automation and robots.

62148905
I have an unrelated pet peeve of mine. I'm sick and fucking tired of millennials larping as if it's still 2015 and they're paying off student loans on a one-store apartment.

I applaud the ones that understand that they're getting old. The delusion can't last forever. It's the ones that pretend they're still rocking the boat despite having a house and a savings account on the 6 digits.

62149041
>>62144801
poor people do not understand rich people's problems and rich people do not understand poor people's problems.

there is no solution. they will always be in conflict with each other.

rich people never have or have forgotten what its like to be poor so they repeat the same regurgitated bullshit about how poor people are stupid and lazy etc.
some of it is true but much of it is also out of touch. it definitely helps to have a network of other rich people for opportunities a poor person will just never encounter in their entire lives.
access to favorable loans etc.

on the opposite you have poor people who have a bunch of prejudice about how rich people got rich in the first place and what it takes to actually stay rich and a lot of that is also out of touch.

again, there is no solution.
in general, people are too full of themselves on both sides.

62149175
>>62148905
Nigger the only thing I have six figure of is dead and yes I am still paying student loans.

62149402
>>62148905
>dis nigga over here doesn't understand arrested development from 20 years of bait and switch careers
I'm not in that boat; I have a PhD and a stable career (that pays like half of what it should), but I get what my peers are going through. Everything past 9/11 has been a downhill slide into a cratering society/civilization and constant economic downturn plastered over by the money printer. There's very few opportunities to bounce back if you get kneecapped with a layoff/accident/divorce/etc since its hard enough to get started with anything in the first place. Some of those people admittedly never tried, and they're losers; the ones that just got both barrels of reality to the face and can't muster the energy to start over I have some amount of sympathy towards

62150041
>>62144850
My jewish professor told me immigrants grow the economy tho

62150364
>>62149402
>Everything past 9/11 has been a downhill slide into a cratering society/civilization and constant economic downturn plastered over by the money printer.
I wouldn't say constant but yes.
>the ones that just got both barrels of reality to the face and can't muster the energy to start over I have some amount of sympathy towards
You lost me at "can't muster the energy to start over".

62150531
>>62150364
Tell me that again when you've spent 20 years pouring your life into something and have it fall out from underneath you. Its easier to paint yourself as a never gonna die hero before it happens to you. I've seen it mess some people up pretty hard

62150834
>>62150531
>Its easier to paint yourself as a never gonna die hero
I was thinking more of a normal person but obviously all you understand is the delusions of grandure the baby boomers raised you towards but could never meet.

62151920
>>62150834
All that made up headcanon is cool and whatever, but congratulations on never having been close to genuine hardship. I hope it stays that way for you

62153024
>>62148493
good post anon
so true

62153123
>>62148493
yep when I was a kid my parents moved us out from a somewhat sketchy area that is now very expensive. If we just stayed then they'd be far more wealthy on paper from the house alone. But in reality that's 30+ year hindsight and they simply valued safety and freedom rather than oh boy I hope my house goes up. Something I don't think our younger generations understand because of narcissism no desire for children. Everyone wants generational wealth but will have no generational legacy to even give it to

62153183
>>62149041
>poor people do not understand rich people's problems and rich people do not understand poor people's problems.
I think there's a cultural dimension too. White progressive types especially women, want to see uneducated white people as their lessors but brown people as simply victims of the system. Even objectively bad cultural aspects get excused and never addressed. So tyrone shoots andre over some trivial bullshit it's white people's fault. And when non-white people immigrate here after generations of being systematic exploiters ie Brahmins (like Kamala) these progressively minded ppl see absolutely nothing wrong there. But they despise Mr Bubba 4th gen redneck with alcoholic pregnant mom and dad who beat the shit out of him growing up

62153826
>>62144801
>Pretends random deaths in the family aren't a drain
>Pretends chronic disease doesn't drain on families
>Pretends random false allegations don't fuck with families
>Pretends everyone is lucky and nobody ever gets screwed by the hands of fate
I literally never feel envy (never makes sense imo) but it never cease to annoy me when richfags like OP larp like luck doesn't exist. Just thank God he was merciful to you and stop telling me urine is rain.

OP

62154305

62154308

62154317
>>62154280

62154318
>>62154280

62154325
>>62154307

Way too much on the noose

62154348
>>621542971

Did she? This is all I could find.
https://www.hrpa.ca/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/Decision-and-Reasons-TS.pdf
She went in front of a board of inquiry and had to take an "appropriate professional behavior" class, but only got a light slap on the wrist because of her ongoing medical problem.

item.title: >zero investing experience >doesn't even work in finance >worked for SBF >fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming >giv… — 46 replies — 11.1/hr

>worked for SBF
>fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming
>given billions of dollars to play with just...because

goycattle really have no idea what is going on in the world

Early replies (first 3)

62149920
>>62149919
if you bought NVDA youd be retired. just get better.

62149927
>>62149920
the man in the picture has puts on nvidia

stay confused and goy

62149991
>>62149919
I hate how all rich white dudes have the same long horse face, 6’2ish, lanky, and probably a long penis to boot.

They always look like this guy and I can’t fucking stand it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62150490
>>62149919
Are we sure....HE....exists?
Fucker LOOKS like he was born from AI parents.

62150535
>>62150489
Every fucking German surname in history except for Hitler or Rommel doubles as deep cover for kikes. So I ask again... what kind of pilpul tactic is this where you pretend to be white to generate goodwill on a basketweaving forum for some rando from sbf's gang who is almost certainly a kike?

62150657
>>62150489
His lips are too plump to be white.

62150665
>>62150535
He is white. There are many rich, corrupt, libtarded whites who aren't jews. Evidently his fiance/wife or whatever is a kike though (white passing one thoughbeit).
t. gigaracist

62150731
>>62150657
Its not the lips its the eyes.

62151498
>>62150490
he is real.
and his fund too

62151503
>>62149919
At the end of the day its just numbers and people who are good with numbers can succeed in any field that has them

62151547
>>62150731
the teeth too, not white. look at the main guy in that scrubs show

62151563
It's ok a I'm a just a teenage millennial there's still time for me to make it like him

62151569
>>62150731
Jews typically have sunken eyes retard
The audacity of this fucking guy coming to my board not knowing his Jewish phenotype

62151573
>>62149919
absolutely revolting physiognomy.

62151697
>>62151569
Its not the sunken eyes either, you pseudo physiognomist. Study Carl Huter.

62151710
>>62149919
>x5.5 in a year
sounds like poor risk management, this guy's going broke sooner or later

62151827
>As of 2025, Aschenbrenner is engaged to Avital Balwit, the chief of staff to the CEO at Anthropic.

Aryans of the West and East are finally reuniting.

62151842
>>62149991
Tall white dudes don't have long dicks, it's usually medium height white dudes. Tall white dudes do have big balls though.

62151844
>>62150489
>Patrilineal name transfer
>Matrilineal ethnicity transfer

62152079
>>62151827
>Aryans
>Avital Balwit
Balwit is eastern euro jewish.

>Aschenbrenner
Coal burner.
It's like pottery.

62152134
>>62149991
He is a homosexual German, though.

62152257
>>62149991
>sees a White person
>seethes and talks about his penis

Serious question: why are brown/black/yellow people like this?

62152274
>>62150535
It's very easy to tell Yiddish names from actual German names if you know German. Jews were not allowed to learn crafts so they never have names like
>Schneider
>Müller
>Jäger
Instead, they usually combined words of nature to create surnames
>Rosenberg (rose mountain)
>Goldfarb (golden color)
>Grünspan (green chip)
The presence of -'stein' is no clear indicator either. Is it the name of an actual place like
>Regenstein
>Wallenstein
>Fürstenstein
? Then it's not a jew. If it's something like
>Goldstein
>Feinstein
it's clearly a jew.

62152340
CCI is what actually measures how main street is doing. How the fuck does trump still have plebe support at this point?

https://www.advisorperspectives.com/dshort/updates/2026/03/31/two-measures-of-consumer-attitudes-march-2026

62152354
you should be glad he's an actual german and not a kike.
>zero investing experience
see below
>doesn't even work in finance
see below again
>worked for SBF
here's the finance experience you claim doesn't exist. retard. he was smart enough to see the writing on the walls and got out while he still could
>fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming
so he pissed off a top kike? whats the problem here?
>given billions of dollars to play with just...because
so he pissed off a top kike? and went off and did his own thing with apparently great success? whats the problem here?

62152374
>>62152354
>What's the problem here
The problem is I want a billion dollars and do not want other people to have a billion dollars. This kid can get fucked, I should be getting prime teen pussy not this fucking zoomer faggot

62152385
>>62152257
My exes friend had a bf like this. He had the long horse face sunken eyes and a 12 inch penis. I had to dump my ex because she was constantly trying to pursue him. It got to the point where our mutual friend was asking me to tell her to stop hitting on him.

It was unreal man. This guy mogged me to death.

62152492
>>62149919
I wonder if he used AI to influence his decisions... The answer is yes, he did.

62152567
>>62150485
How can you be a virgin at 6’.5’’, even as a lanklet you should still easily get dates as being tall makes up for ugliness, also you got big dick so don’t be insecure about it. Girth matters most and you got girth

62152773
>>62152385
Grim

62152801
>>62152274
This. Anons need to learn the difference between jews and Germans fr fr no cap. It's not even hard once you learn the patterns. Also everyone ITT thinking he looks jewish is also retarded. Read some legit nationalsocialist era race science before you pass judgment. This snownigger has a German name and looks white, just not with the best physiognomy (he is a rich libshit who cavorts with jews after all). I had a friend growing up who looked very similar, also a nerdy libtard type who was 0% kike, just anglo.

62153742
>>62151827
So his fiancee fired him? Lol

62153750
>>62151842
not true i am 6ft but my weiner is like 7 and 5/8 inch on a good day so not that long

item.title: WTFWT — 36 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTFWT

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-04-24 20:39 UTC

OP

Yea, I’m thinking we’re back

Early replies (first 3)

62150553
Stop buying I get paid on Monday all I want is ONE more cheap buy
Please

62150563
>>62150529
Yeah I'm not falling for that KEK there's probably 6 million people who still have a stack who would gladly sell if they saw this thing go up to even a penny.

62150572
>>62150563
The thing is literally everyone who bought a million forgot about it a long long time ago. This will fly to $0.01 under the radar very fast

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62151006
>>62150529
Until it starts dropping zeros anybody shilling this is a confirmed jeetarino.

62151008
>>62150899
No, these threads feel like guerilla fighters laying low accumulating the best decentralized finance technology bar none. So many coins have become exactly what they once hated so they could integrate with tradfi.

62151013
>>62150899
yes lots of jeets push this coin but desu there's very few people even holding it at all.
I have a sui stack because i think we're gonna have a real alt run soon, but only like a $4k bag.
My honest thesis here is that when VET rugged, I put $2k in and made about $170k off of it in 2021. This coin is likely(imo) going to see some crazy shit like that, and then tank again for another 7 years. So I will 100% cash out at any reasonable gains but I have a good feeling about it.

62151017
>>62150529
I still hodl 1mil as a lotto ticket/novelty but I doubt it's ever going anywhere. This was easily the biggest letdown of the past cycle, it was a straight shot to $1, all they had to do was burn the excess. CZ could have easily helped send this shit to the moon but he decided to be a limpdick and leave it all to rot.

62151136
>>62151093
crypto is now like horse betting. some of these crypto blockchain/oracle technologies will become integrated with future platforms. some tokens will have a meaningful role down the line and thus become mainstream.
not gonna sugar coat it, capital has moved on to other pastures AI, quantum, hell even trading cards ate cryptos lunch lol.
this fate is well-deserved for 2 reasons. the crypto speculators front-ran the profits too vigorously and demolished any semblance of expected value. also most people have matured though pain and realized that tangible assets are better than total loss
